title: nastanthus spathulatus
intro: Nastanthus spathulatus, also known as the "wild jasmine", is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Nastanthus spathulatus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
